prompt_text: Baremetrics vs ChartMogul: which is better?
response:
Baremetrics is generally better if you want an **all‑in‑one growth toolkit** (metrics + forecasting + dunning + cancellation insights) and you’re past roughly **$10k MRR**, while ChartMogul is better if you want **more powerful analytics/segmentation and a generous free tier** (up to $10k MRR) and/or have a more complex billing stack.[1][2][3]

### Core difference in positioning

- **Baremetrics**: Focuses on being an **all‑in‑one subscription growth tool**: core SaaS metrics plus **revenue recovery (dunning), cancellation insights, and full financial forecasting**.[1][3][4]  
- **ChartMogul**: Focuses on **deep subscription analytics**: more charts, cohorts, data editing and auditing tools, and flexible segmentation across many integrations, but with **little/no built‑in dunning or cancellation tooling**.[2][3]

Both cover core metrics very well (MRR, ARR, churn, LTV, cohorts).[1][3]

### Feature comparison (high level)

| Area | Baremetrics | ChartMogul |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Core SaaS metrics | **Yes** – full | **Yes** – full[1][2][3] |
| Segmentation & cohorts | Strong, unlimited segments & dashboards[1] | **More powerful/advanced**; more charts, cohorts, data editing[2] |
| Financial forecasting | **Full financial forecasting** (P&L, runway, burn) via Forecast+ (QuickBooks/Xero integration)[1][3] | **Subscription revenue modeling only** (projects ARR under scenarios)[1] |
| Failed payment recovery (dunning) | **Yes – Recover**: automated dunning and revenue recovery (Stripe‑native)[1][3] | **No native dunning**; you need separate tools[1][3] |
| Cancellation insights | **Yes – built‑in widget, reason collection, flows**[1][4] | **No native cancellation insights**[1][3][4] |
| CRM | No native CRM; integrates with others like HubSpot[1][3] | **Native CRM** on all plans including free[1][2] |
| Data control & editing | Less emphasis on data editing | **Strong tools for data control, editing, auditing and transparency**[2] |
| Integrations breadth | Strong for Stripe‑native SaaS; supports Stripe, Braintree, Recurly, Apple, Google, Shopify Partners, etc.[3][4] | Strong multi‑source support, including PayPal, GoCardless, Chargify, Zuora, etc., plus major billing systems and enrichment tools.[2][4] |

### Pricing: when is each cheaper?

From the vendor comparisons, **pricing flips depending on your MRR**:

- **Pre‑$10k MRR**  
  - ChartMogul has a **free plan up to $10k MRR** with core analytics and 1 CRM seat.[1][3]  
  - Baremetrics starts at **$49/mo** (Accelerator tier) and has no permanent free plan.[1][3]  
  - For *pure metrics* at this stage, **ChartMogul is usually the better choice**.

- **Roughly $10k–$30k MRR**  
  - Baremetrics claims a **pricing advantage exactly in the $10k–$30k MRR range**, while also adding forecasting, dunning, and cancellation tooling that ChartMogul lacks.[1]  
  - If you now care about revenue recovery and investor‑grade financials, **Baremetrics tends to give more capability for less money** than ChartMogul’s paid tiers.[1]

- **Above ~$40k MRR**  
  - Baremetrics claims **ChartMogul becomes more than twice as expensive beyond $40k MRR** under typical pricing assumptions.[4]  
  - ChartMogul’s own article argues it now has the better price‑to‑features ratio since Baremetrics’ 2022 price increase.[2]  
  - Net: you need to price your actual MRR and feature needs against both calculators, but Baremetrics argues it remains cheaper at higher MRR if you’d otherwise have to bolt on separate tools for dunning and forecasting.[1][3][4]

### Support, UX, and ease of use

- **Baremetrics**  
  - Emphasizes **fast, hands‑on support** (unlimited calls, ~2‑minute chat response) and an **intuitive interface** that many users praise.[1][4]  
  - Built to be easy for **Stripe‑native SaaS founders** who want “it just works” dashboards plus actionable tools.[3][4]

- **ChartMogul**
